description: Chat Performance Optimization — context caching, LLM pre-warming, greeting cache, context trimming, NPC-NPC conversation pool, per-line streaming, eavesdropping UI, proximity greetings, tiered LLM routing, speculative generation, response caching, template fallback, batch NPC-NPC generation, adaptive quality degradation, walking interrupts, TTS pipeline optimization, and automated tests

userStories.title: Wire context caching into ws-bridge
userStories.description: As a player, I need buildContext() results cached between follow-up turns so that redundant DB queries are eliminated and response times improve.
acceptanceCriteria: In ws-bridge.ts handleTextInput(), check ConversationContextCache before calling buildContext(); use cached context if characterId unchanged
After buildContext() on cache miss, store result via ConversationContextCache.set() using chatKey() format
Cache invalidation triggers: CEFR level change, quest accepted/completed, relationship change, world time advance >1 game hour (via GameEventBus)
Follow-up turns only rebuild lightweight parts (conversation history); keep expensive parts cached (personality, world, language directives, relationships)
Add context_cache_hit/miss metric to conversation-metrics.ts MetricStage type
Verify ConversationContextCache.append() works for adding messages without full rebuild
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: ConversationContextCache exists at conversation-context-cache.ts with LRU (max 200, 30-min TTL) and chatKey(), but ws-bridge.ts calls buildContext() unconditionally at lines 100-111. Expected: -0.5s per follow-up turn.

userStories.title: Pre-warm LLM context on player proximity
userStories.description: As a player, I need the conversation pipeline pre-warmed when I approach an NPC so that opening chat has minimal cold-start latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Add player_near_npc event to GameEventBus, fired when player enters 10-unit radius of any NPC
Detect proximity in NPCInitiatedConversationController.evaluateApproach() which already iterates nearby NPCs
Create pre-warm endpoint/message: build context, build system prompt, optionally pre-load into Gemini
In ws-bridge.ts add pre_warm message handler alongside handleTextInput/handleAudioChunk
Client sends pre-warm when player within range of NPC not talked to in last 60 seconds
Debounce: only pre-warm for nearest NPC, cancel if player moves away
Add pre_warm metric stage to conversation-metrics.ts
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: LLM cold start is 1-4+ seconds. NPCInitiatedConversationController already tracks proximity at 15-unit radius. Expected: -1-2s first token latency.

userStories.title: Implement greeting cache for instant first response
userStories.description: As a player, I need pre-generated NPC greetings served instantly on first interaction so that I don't wait for an LLM round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GreetingCache at server/services/conversation/greeting-cache.ts with Map keyed by worldId:npcId, storing 5 greetings per NPC with time/weather/relationship variants
Batch generation: single LLM prompt generates greetings for multiple NPCs at once with lighter temperature (0.9), short maxTokens (200/NPC)
Trigger generation on world/session load for 20 nearest NPCs, respecting rate limits
In ws-bridge.ts, for NEW conversations serve cached greeting instantly via WebSocket before normal LLM pipeline continues
Integrate with NPCInitiatedConversationController: use cached greetings for onShowGreeting callback
Cache refresh on CEFR level change, quest-relevant NPC content change, or after 1 hour
Greeting language mode matches player CEFR level
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently first NPC message requires full LLM round-trip. Expected: instant first response.

userStories.title: Trim context for follow-up turns
userStories.description: As a developer, I need system prompts trimmed on follow-up turns so that token count and latency are reduced.
acceptanceCriteria: Add turnNumber parameter to buildContext()/buildSystemPrompt() in context-manager.ts
Turn 1: full context (4000 tokens). Turn 2+: condense world description to one line, remove unchanged environment details, condense quest awareness to IDs + current objective only
Keep language learning directives and character personality in full on all turns
Log token counts per section (character, world, environment, quests, language) on turn 1 vs turn 2+
ws-bridge.ts passes turn count from session to buildContext()
Add context_tokens metric to conversation-metrics.ts
Verify 20-message history cap is working correctly
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: context-manager.ts buildSystemPrompt() caps at 4000 tokens but includes full context on every turn. Expected: -0.5s per turn.

userStories.title: Pre-generate NPC-NPC conversation pool
userStories.description: As a player, I need a pool of pre-generated NPC-NPC conversations so that ambient conversations display instantly instead of waiting for LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: Create NpcConversationPool at server/services/conversation/npc-conversation-pool.ts with Map keyed by worldId, capacity 50/world
Methods: add(), take(worldId, npc1Id, npc2Id) with best-match or any-available fallback, size(), isFull()
On world load, async background job generates 15-20 conversations respecting 3-concurrent rate limit
Background replenishment worker every 30 seconds; refill when below 30
In npc-conversation-engine.ts, check pool before LLM call at line 597; fall back to on-demand if empty
Topic diversity via selectTopics() with random NPC pairs; track generated topics
Conversation freshness: deprioritize >1 game-day old, evict >3 game-days
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: No conversation pre-generation exists — HIGH severity bottleneck. Expected: near-instant NPC-NPC display.

userStories.title: Per-line streaming for NPC-NPC conversations
userStories.description: As a player, I need NPC-NPC conversation lines displayed as they generate rather than waiting for the full response so that ambient conversations feel live.
acceptanceCriteria: In npc-conversation-engine.ts lines 597-604, implement incremental line parsing: detect newlines/speaker-change patterns and emit complete lines immediately
Add optional onLineReady(speaker, line, lineIndex) callback to initiateConversation()
Client receives each line via WebSocket and displays as speech bubble above speaking NPC immediately
Add npc_npc_line WebSocket message type in ws-bridge.ts with { speaker, speakerId, line, lineIndex }
TTS per-line if enabled (SentenceAccumulator pattern)
Keep full accumulation path for relationship updates and persistence via parseLlmConversation()
Add npc_npc_first_line latency metric
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: npc-conversation-engine.ts accumulates full response into fullResponse before parsing — HIGH severity.

userStories.title: Eavesdropping UI for nearby NPC-NPC conversations
userStories.description: As a player, I need to overhear nearby NPC-NPC conversations in a side panel so that I get passive language exposure and can complete eavesdropping quests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create BabylonEavesdropPanel at shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonEavesdropPanel.ts: semi-transparent side panel (right, 25% width) with speaker names, typewriter line effect, hover-to-translate via HoverTranslationSystem
In BabylonGame.ts NPC update loop, detect two NPCs within 10 units in active ambient conversation and subscribe player to line stream
Vocabulary learning: hovering over words records to PersistentLanguageTracker
Quest integration: emit eavesdrop_completed event on GameEventBus with npc1Id, npc2Id, topic, durationSeconds
Audio: TTS at volume scaled by distance if enabled
Visual indicator (ear icon/speech bubble) above chatting NPCs when eavesdroppable
Keybind V to explicitly open eavesdrop panel; rate limit events once per conversation per NPC pair
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: No eavesdropping UI exists. QuestCompletionEngine already references an 'eavesdrop' case at line 432.

userStories.title: Proximity greetings from NPCs
userStories.description: As a player, I need NPCs to occasionally call out when I'm nearby so that the world feels alive and I discover quest-givers naturally.
acceptanceCriteria: Add evaluateCallout() to NPCInitiatedConversationController running every 30 game-seconds
Callout at 8-unit range with probability: base 20%, +20% extroversion >0.7, +10% quest-bearer, -10% introvert
Serve from GreetingCache instantly — no LLM latency; CEFR-appropriate language
Display as small speech bubble above NPC for 4 seconds
Show '[G] Respond' prompt for 3 seconds; pressing G opens full conversation
Cooldowns: 60s per NPC, 15s global
Quest-bearer NPCs have 50% callout probability with '!' indicator
Use calculateApproachProbability() logic as reference for personality factors
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Analysis identifies 'No NPC-to-player initiation' as a gap.

userStories.title: Tiered LLM routing (fast/full models)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need greetings and simple exchanges routed to a faster model so that latency is reduced without quality loss on complex conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: In gemini-provider.ts, add model tier support: 'fast' (lower maxTokens 256, temperature 0.7, faster model) and 'full' (current)
Create conversation-classifier.ts: FAST for greetings, simple social, NPC-NPC ambient, non-quest follow-ups; FULL for quest conversations, B1+ complex language
Wire classifier into ws-bridge.ts handleTextInput() to determine tier before streamCompletion()
NPC-NPC conversations default to FAST tier unless quest-relevant
Track separate latency distributions for fast/full tiers in conversation-metrics.ts
Fallback: if FAST model unavailable/errors, transparently fall back to FULL
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: gemini-provider.ts uses single model for everything. Expected: 50-70% latency reduction for simple exchanges.

userStories.title: Speculative generation for likely opening messages
userStories.description: As a player, I need the system to pre-generate responses to my likely opening messages so that common greetings get near-zero latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SpeculativeResponseCache at server/services/conversation/speculative-cache.ts with nested Map: worldId:npcId → canonical message → { response, audio?, timestamp }
Define likely openings by CEFR: A1 ['hello','hi','bonjour','who are you'], A2 add ['comment allez-vous','je cherche'], B1+ add ['tell me about','can you help me']
On pre-warm proximity trigger, generate top 3 likely responses in parallel using FAST tier
In ws-bridge.ts, canonicalize first player message and check speculative cache; if hit send immediately, kick off real generation in background, correct if significantly different
Pre-generate TTS audio for speculative responses
Cache expiration: 5 minutes, max 10 NPC entries
Track hit rate metric
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Expected: near-zero latency for first response in ~40-60% of conversations.

userStories.title: Response caching for identical exchanges
userStories.description: As a developer, I need identical context+message pairs cached so that common greetings don't require repeated LLM calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ResponseCache at server/services/conversation/response-cache.ts with LRU (max 500, 15-min TTL)
Cache key: hash of (npcId + CEFR level + canonical message + turn number)
Only cache generic, context-independent messages (greetings, farewells, common questions); NOT quest-related or long messages
Wire into ws-bridge.ts: check cache after building context but before streamCompletion(); record response_cache_hit metric
Store up to 3 responses per key for variation; randomly select when serving
Cache scoped per NPC + CEFR level (shared across players)
Add cache stats to GET /api/metrics/conversation endpoint
Typecheck passes, tests pass

userStories.title: Template fallback for NPC-NPC conversations
userStories.description: As a player, I need instant template conversations shown while LLM generates so that NPC-NPC conversations never have visible latency.
acceptanceCriteria: Expand template library from 4 to 20+ templates covering all 12 topic types with 3-5 variants each
Add bilingual templates per CEFR tier
Implement show-then-replace: return template instantly AND start LLM generation; client displays template, transitions to LLM version if still in progress
Decision flow in initiateConversation(): check pool → template-with-replacement → synchronous LLM; return source type
Topic-appropriate template selection matching NPC personality profiles
Track template_served vs llm_served vs pool_served metrics
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: generateFallbackConversation() at line 431 has 4 hardcoded templates, used only on LLM failure. Expected: instant display for 100% of NPC-NPC conversations.

userStories.title: Batch NPC-NPC conversation generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need multiple NPC-NPC conversations generated in a single LLM call so that pool replenishment is 3-5x faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Create batchGenerateConversations() in npc-conversation-engine.ts taking array of { npc1, npc2, topic, exchangeCount }
Build single prompt with character roster at top, conversation requests with delimiters
Parse batch response splitting on delimiters, applying parseLlmConversation() per section
Handle partial failures: salvage valid conversations from malformed batches
Wire into pool replenishment: batch instead of one-at-a-time; use FAST model tier
Optimal batch size 3-5; reduce if parse failure >10%
Rate limiting: batch counts as 1 slot, add separate maxBatchesPerWorld limit
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Expected: 3-5x throughput improvement, pool fill time ~2-3 minutes instead of ~10.

userStories.title: Enhanced adaptive quality degradation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need multi-level quality degradation covering TTS, context size, and model tier so that the system gracefully handles high load.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace binary _degraded flag with quality tiers: FULL (<1500ms p95), STANDARD (1500-3000ms), REDUCED (3000-5000ms), MINIMAL (>5000ms)

FULL: full visemes + TTS + full context + FULL model. STANDARD: slim context on follow-ups. REDUCED: simplified visemes, TTS first sentence only, FAST model for non-quest. MINIMAL: no visemes/TTS, minimal context, FAST for everything, template fallback
Wire tier into ws-bridge.ts and http-bridge.ts for per-request parameter adjustment
Hysteresis: 10 consecutive measurements above threshold to degrade, 20 below to recover
Add current quality tier to GET /api/metrics/conversation endpoint
Send quality tier to client via WebSocket for UI adjustment
Log tier transitions with triggering metrics
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently only degrades viseme quality (15→5 shapes) when p95 >4000ms. evaluateAdaptiveQuality() at line 149.

userStories.title: Walking interrupt callouts
userStories.description: As a player, I need brief NPC one-liners as I walk through town so that the world feels alive without forcing conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Track player velocity; isPlayerWalking() returns true when moved >2 units in last 2 seconds
walkingInterruptCheck() runs every 1-2 seconds when walking; checks NPCs within 6 units player is moving past
Probability: 10% base, +10% extroverts, +15% quest-bearers
1-sentence max callouts from greeting cache or separate phrase pool; CEFR-appropriate
Display as small brief speech bubble (2 seconds, smaller font), NO TTS audio
Show faded '[G] Stop and chat' for 3 seconds; G opens full conversation
Rate limits: 1 interrupt per 8 seconds global, 3 minutes per NPC
Vocabulary reinforcement: include recently-learned words; settings toggle On/Reduced/Off
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Distinct from proximity greetings — these fire when player is MOVING THROUGH an area.

userStories.title: Optimize SentenceAccumulator and TTS pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reduced per-sentence TTS overhead and parallel synthesis so that audio responses start faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Optimize SentenceAccumulator.push() boundary detection: handle abbreviations (Mr., Dr.), ellipses, French guillemets, inverted punctuation
Speculative TTS: start accumulating sentence N+1 while sentence N synthesizes; verify parallel dispatch with maxConcurrency=5
Pre-synthesize TTS audio for cached greetings at cache time, not serving time
Optimize cleanForSpeech(): compile regexes once at module load; consolidate with http-bridge.ts cleanForTTS() if duplicated
TTS result caching: same sentence + voice ID within 5 minutes returns cached audio
Track tts_queue_depth metric; if consistently >3 sentences, flag for tuning
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: streaming-chat.ts processStreamWithTTS() uses SentenceAccumulator with maxConcurrency=5. Expected: -0.5-1s perceived latency.

userStories.title: Automated tests for chat performance systems
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test suite covering all chat performance improvements so that regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: conversation-context-cache.test.ts: hit/miss, TTL expiry, LRU eviction, chatKey(), append(), invalidation triggers
greeting-cache.test.ts: batch generation, retrieval by NPC, CEFR language ratios, refresh, empty fallback
npc-conversation-pool.test.ts: capacity 50, best-match take(), any-available fallback, replenishment trigger at 30, freshness expiry, topic diversity
conversation-classifier.test.ts: tier routing for greetings/quest/NPC-NPC/CEFR levels, edge cases
adaptive-quality.test.ts: tier computation from p95, hysteresis, tier effects, recovery
speculative-cache.test.ts: hit/miss, 5-min expiry, NPC+CEFR scoping, hit rate tracking
batch-generation.test.ts: prompt construction, response parsing, partial failure handling, rate limit accounting
Integration test: approach NPC → pre-warm → greeting → 3-turn convo with context caching → NPC-NPC nearby from pool
All tests pass
